game_name: Quest for Glory: So You Want to Be a Hero

danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to valley inhabitants; indicates unsafe zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Kobolds and other fantasy creatures visible in specific locations
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazards; areas controlled by hostile forces

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual transformation of the baronet into bear form; environmental decay
danger_signals.meaning: Baba Yaga's magical corruption; world under supernatural threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Scenery adjusted to dark palette; changed lighting
danger_signals.meaning: Increased danger periods; certain enemies more active during night

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss entity with distinctive appearance in final confrontation
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate source of valley's corruption; primary antagonistic force

safety_signals.meaning: Sources of aid, supplies, and knowledge; safe interaction points

safety_signals.meaning: Safe respite; restoration of health and fatigue

safety_signals.visual_form: Magical sanctuary space with benevolent faery association
safety_signals.meaning: Divine protection; temporary sanctuary from threats

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Baron's authority; daughter Elsa as symbol of noble lineage
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Town of Spielburg; baron's castle; agricultural lands

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Brigand sprites distinct from town guards; enemy behavior patterns

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Witch aesthetic; curse magic visual effects
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Unique boss sprite; magical aura or curse manifestations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Cursed terrain; enchanted locations; locations of magical corruption

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Wizard imagery; faery associations; familiar creatures

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Monster-infested landscape; brigand control of wilderness; environmental decay; darkened or corrupted visual palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs in distress; reduced movement by civilians; brigands actively patrolling; monsters encountered frequently

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gradual environmental restoration; specific locations becoming safer after quests completed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs showing gratitude; fewer monsters in cleared areas; brigand activity reduced in solved regions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spielburg Valley returned to prosperity; visual transformation of landscape; Elsa freed from brigand leader form
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Celebration of Hero; NPCs in normal state; threat entities eliminated; departure sequence on magic carpet

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scenery adjusted with palette shifts; lighting changes; environmental mood alteration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different enemy encounters; certain NPCs unavailable at night; fatigue mechanics affecting player character

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UI indicators (if visible); character sprite visual degradation or animation changes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced player movement speed; inability to perform actions; necessity of rest and food consumption

environmental_storytelling.example: Areas ravaged by monsters show environmental decay; brigand-controlled territories have distinct visual markers indicating hostile occupation
Baron's son visible as bear; Elsa's dual identity as both missing daughter and brigand leader reveals through gameplay revelation
Lost items, spell ingredients, and side quest locations visually distinct; fantasy creatures (dryad, hermit, Meeps) inhabit specific narrative-relevant areas
Erana's Peace shrine exists as visual safe-zone; wizard Erasmus and familiar Fenrus appear in locations signaling magical intervention
Day-night cycle creates visual mood; scenery adjusts to time-of-day affecting both danger perception and environmental storytelling

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrolling wilderness; attacking player character; leader (Elsa) commanding brigand operations
Inhabiting specific areas; increasing in frequency in cursed zones; attacking on encounter
Immobilized or restricted to castle; seeking hero to break curse; showing desperation through dialogue
Remaining in safe town locations; offering quests to aid in valley restoration; showing relief as quests complete
Maintaining curse; commanding monsters and brigands indirectly; confronted in final mandatory encounter
Movement restrictions; inability to perform actions; requiring food and rest mechanics

notes: Quest for Glory 1 uses a satirical tone with fantasy folklore sources. World-state communication relies heavily on environmental decay contrast (cursed vs. restored), time-of-day mechanics affecting both visuals and danger levels, and NPC behavior shifts reflecting valley's condition. Class selection (Fighter, Magic User, Thief) determines initial equipment and quest accessibility, creating faction-like distinctions through gameplay rather than explicit visual faction markers. The game's open-ended design allows player exploration of all zones, with danger signals primarily conveyed through enemy sprite encounters and environmental visual degradation rather than explicit UI warnings. Curse manifestation (bear transformation, brigand leadership revelation) serves as central political/narrative signal.
